Main Page   Modules   Compound List   File List   Compound Members   File Members  

csyn.h

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 #ifndef  _CSYN_H
00002 #define  _CSYN_H
00003 /*
00004 ** CSyn - Synthesis Engine
00005 **
00006 ** Author: Phil Burk
00007 ** Copyright 1996-2004 Phil Burk
00008 ** All Rights Reserved.
00009 */
00010 
00011 #ifdef WIN32
00012 #include <windows.h>
00013 #else
00014 #include <ctype.h>
00015 #endif
00016 
00017 /* CSyn uses PortAudio as its audio interface library.
00018  * For more information, see http://www.portaudio.com
00019  */
00020 #include "portaudio.h"
00021 
00022 /*************************************************************
00023 ***********  CSyn.h data types  ****************************
00024 *************************************************************/
00025 #ifndef int8
00026         typedef char  int8;
00027         typedef unsigned char  uint8;
00028 #endif
00029 #ifndef int16
00030         typedef short  int16;
00031         typedef unsigned short  uint16;
00032 #endif
00033 #ifndef int32
00034         typedef long   int32;
00035         typedef unsigned long  uint32;
00036 #endif
00037 
00038 /** Result code datatype for CSyn.
00039  * Generally negative values indicate an error has occured.
00040  */
00041 typedef int32   CSynErr;
00042 /** CSyn resources are referenced using an integer token. */
00043 typedef int32   CSynToken;
00044 
00045 /** Opaque pointer to CSyn internal data. */
00046 typedef void   *CSynContext;
00047 
00048 /** The names of unit generator and their ports are passed as integers
00049  * generated by hashing the name text.
00050  */
00051 typedef uint32  CSynName;
00052 typedef uint32  CSynUnitType;
00053 
00054 
00055 /** A segmented envelope reads an array of EnvelopeFrame structures.
00056  * This is used with CSyn_WriteEnvelope().
00057  */
00058 typedef struct EnvelopeFrame_s
00059 {
00060 /** Time it takes to get to the target value from previous value.  */
00061         double      env_Duration; 
00062 /** Target value for this segment. */
00063         double      env_Value;
00064 } EnvelopeFrame;
00065 
00066 /** Rate of calculation. */
00067 typedef enum  CSyn_CalculationRate_
00068 {
00069         CSYN_RATE_AUDIO = 0,
00070         CSYN_RATE_CONTROL /* Not currently implemented. */
00071 } CSyn_CalculationRate;
00072 
00073 /********************************************************************/
00074 /* Define hash values for PORT and UNIT names. */
00075 /* Do NOT edit. Generated automatically by gen_hash.c */
00076 #define CSYN_PORT_A0                             (0x000001C1)
00077 #define CSYN_PORT_A1                             (0x000001C9)
00078 #define CSYN_PORT_A2                             (0x000001D1)
00079 #define CSYN_PORT_A3                             (0x000001D9)
00080 #define CSYN_PORT_A4                             (0x000001E1)
00081 #define CSYN_PORT_Alpha                          (0x0006FFA1)
00082 #define CSYN_PORT_Amplitude                      (0x02158EC1)
00083 #define CSYN_PORT_B0                             (0x000001C2)
00084 #define CSYN_PORT_B1                             (0x000001CA)
00085 #define CSYN_PORT_B2                             (0x000001D2)
00086 #define CSYN_PORT_B3                             (0x000001DA)
00087 #define CSYN_PORT_B4                             (0x000001E2)
00088 #define CSYN_PORT_BandPass                       (0x020267B0)
00089 #define CSYN_PORT_Bandwidth                      (0x01CCDE32)
00090 #define CSYN_PORT_Beta                           (0x0000E26A)
00091 #define CSYN_PORT_BusInput                       (0x02139392)
00092 #define CSYN_PORT_BusOutput                      (0x020216D4)
00093 #define CSYN_PORT_Current                        (0x020E546B)
00094 #define CSYN_PORT_Confidence                     (0x01CDB4C7)
00095 #define CSYN_PORT_Damping                        (0x01DA8E8C)
00096 #define CSYN_PORT_Data                           (0x0000E24C)
00097 #define CSYN_PORT_Delay                          (0x0008706C)
00098 #define CSYN_PORT_Fade                           (0x0000E64E)
00099 #define CSYN_PORT_Frequency                      (0x01F2D76C)
00100 #define CSYN_PORT_Gain                           (0x0000F98F)
00101 #define CSYN_PORT_Gamma                          (0x0007088F)
00102 #define CSYN_PORT_Gate                           (0x0000EA4F)
00103 #define CSYN_PORT_HalfLife                       (0x01D22B1A)
00104 #define CSYN_PORT_HighPass                       (0x02026E36)
00105 #define CSYN_PORT_Input                          (0x000849B9)
00106 #define CSYN_PORT_InputA                         (0x0028C9B9)
00107 #define CSYN_PORT_InputB                         (0x002949B9)
00108 #define CSYN_PORT_InputC                         (0x0029C9B9)
00109 #define CSYN_PORT_LowerLimit                     (0x01D24CEE)
00110 #define CSYN_PORT_LowPass                        (0x020C5184)
00111 #define CSYN_PORT_Output                         (0x004250F7)
00112 #define CSYN_PORT_OutputA                        (0x014650F7)
00113 #define CSYN_PORT_OutputB                        (0x014A50F7)
00114 #define CSYN_PORT_OutputPulse                    (0x01842621)
00115 #define CSYN_PORT_Pan                            (0x00001ED8)
00116 #define CSYN_PORT_Period                         (0x0039E1F8)
00117 #define CSYN_PORT_Phase                          (0x000751D0)
00118 #define CSYN_PORT_Q                              (0x00000051)
00119 #define CSYN_PORT_Rate                           (0x0000EA5A)
00120 #define CSYN_PORT_ResetLevel                     (0x01C26776)
00121 #define CSYN_PORT_Resonance                      (0x01F06550)
00122 #define CSYN_PORT_Select                         (0x0041187B)
00123 #define CSYN_PORT_SetLevel                       (0x01D60953)
00124 #define CSYN_PORT_Slope                          (0x00074F73)
00125 #define CSYN_PORT_Stiffness                      (0x01D25249)
00126 #define CSYN_PORT_Table                          (0x000743DC)
00127 #define CSYN_PORT_Time                           (0x0000E8DC)
00128 #define CSYN_PORT_TriggerOutput                  (0x02867E12)
00129 #define CSYN_PORT_TriggerPass                    (0x0203E854)
00130 #define CSYN_PORT_UpperLimit                     (0x01D24B3F)
00131 #define CSYN_PORT_Width                          (0x0007849F)
00132 #define CSYN_UNIT_Adapt_OneInOneOut              (0x05160329)
00133 #define CSYN_UNIT_Adapt_TwoInDualOut             (0x0513408F)
00134 #define CSYN_UNIT_Adapt_DualInTwoOut             (0x0566FD37)
00135 #define CSYN_UNIT_Bus_Read                       (0x01BC7F72)
00136 #define CSYN_UNIT_Bus_Write                      (0x01E355E2)
00137 #define CSYN_UNIT_Circuit                        (0x020CB60B)
00138 #define CSYN_UNIT_Channel_In                     (0x01EA9411)
00139 #define CSYN_UNIT_Channel_Out                    (0x01EC67F1)
00140 #define CSYN_UNIT_Delay_One                      (0x0173F798)
00141 #define CSYN_UNIT_Delay_Basic                    (0x0141B8DE)
00142 #define CSYN_UNIT_Delay_AllPass                  (0x01BF0404)
00143 #define CSYN_UNIT_Delay_Interpolated             (0x053E880C)
00144 #define CSYN_UNIT_Envelope_Linear                (0x057BBDB7)
00145 #define CSYN_UNIT_Envelope_Parabolic             (0x0583AE2B)
00146 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_1o1p                    (0x01BE3660)
00147 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_1o1p1z                  (0x023E5660)
00148 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_1o1z                    (0x01BE5E60)
00149 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_2o2p                    (0x01BE36E2)
00150 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_2o2p2z                  (0x023E76E2)
00151 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_AllPass                 (0x05D4F3D0)
00152 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_BandPass                (0x05C125EE)
00153 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_BandStop                (0x05B485E2)
00154 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_Comb                    (0x01BE1C84)
00155 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_LowPass                 (0x05D4F996)
00156 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_LowShelf                (0x058FE72E)
00157 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_HighPass                (0x05C132FA)
00158 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_HighShelf               (0x05589F9E)
00159 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_PeakingEQ               (0x05716532)
00160 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_StateVariable           (0x06117EDC)
00161 #define CSYN_UNIT_Filter_StateVariableOld        (0x06119B8B)
00162 #define CSYN_UNIT_Lag_Exponential                (0x05829F02)
00163 #define CSYN_UNIT_Lag_Linear                     (0x01F25AEE)
00164 #define CSYN_UNIT_Lag_PeakFollower               (0x05F2D79E)
00165 #define CSYN_UNIT_Lag_Thrust                     (0x02025D2E)
00166 #define CSYN_UNIT_Line_In                        (0x01E35914)
00167 #define CSYN_UNIT_Line_Out                       (0x020259FC)
00168 #define CSYN_UNIT_Logic_Compare                  (0x01B7A332)
00169 #define CSYN_UNIT_Logic_Latch                    (0x01687906)
00170 #define CSYN_UNIT_Logic_SchmidtTrigger           (0x05A5EF32)
00171 #define CSYN_UNIT_Logic_Select                   (0x0192E58E)
00172 #define CSYN_UNIT_Model_MassSpring               (0x0534C2CF)
00173 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_AbsoluteValue             (0x04D89E2F)
00174 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Add                       (0x01B7611D)
00175 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_AddUnsigned               (0x059D9781)
00176 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_CrossFade                 (0x0589D9E3)
00177 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Divide                    (0x01CEADD1)
00178 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_FourWayFade               (0x0544C60F)
00179 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Integrate                 (0x058D830D)
00180 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Maximum                   (0x01C11255)
00181 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Minimum                   (0x01E11241)
00182 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Multiply                  (0x0289DCED)
00183 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_MultiplyAdd               (0x0491EAFD)
00184 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_MultiplyAddUnsigned       (0x05855514)
00185 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_MultiplyUnsigned          (0x061B0E69)
00186 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Pan                       (0x01B2E131)
00187 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_PanControl                (0x05AD5691)
00188 #define CSYN_UNIT_Math_Subtract                  (0x02868559)
00189 #define CSYN_UNIT_Measure_Period                 (0x057371F3)
00190 #define CSYN_UNIT_Measure_Costas                 (0x05DEDA23)
00191 #define CSYN_UNIT_Noise_Pink                     (0x01771138)
00192 #define CSYN_UNIT_Noise_Red                      (0x017EDB10)
00193 #define CSYN_UNIT_Noise_White                    (0x0194A966)
00194 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Impulse                    (0x01FDBFD1)
00195 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_ImpulseBL                  (0x0251FFD1)
00196 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Pulse                      (0x01F061DD)
00197 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_PulseBL                    (0x01F1B2DD)
00198 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Sawtooth                   (0x022199FF)
00199 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_SawtoothBL                 (0x04C399FF)
00200 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Sine                       (0x01F28B71)
00201 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_SineFM                     (0x01F2B651)
00202 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_SinePM                     (0x01F2B6F1)
00203 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Square                     (0x0212C509)
00204 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_SquareBL                   (0x021D4D09)
00205 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Table                      (0x01BEA1CF)
00206 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Triangle                   (0x01F1A5C9)
00207 #define CSYN_UNIT_Osc_TriangleBL                 (0x0493A5C9)
00208 #define CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Read16F1                (0x042426D3)
00209 #define CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Read16F2                (0x042426D7)
00210 #define CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Read16V1                (0x04A426D3)
00211 #define CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Write16F1               (0x03A5F221)
00212 #define CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Write16F2               (0x03A5F241)
00213 #define CSYN_UNIT_Table_Lookup                   (0x0176D52A)
00214 /* End of automatically generated code. */
00215 /********************************************************************/
00216 
00217 /** Define Port types so they can be ORed together into a mask for searching. */
00218 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_INPUT    (1<<0)
00219 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_OUTPUT   (1<<1)
00220 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_VARIABLE (1<<2)
00221 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_CHANNEL  (1<<3)
00222 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_TABLE    (1<<4)
00223 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_BUSINPUT (1<<5)
00224 #define CSYN_PORT_TYPE_BUSOUTPUT (1<<6)
00225 
00226 typedef enum CSyn_StatusLevels_e
00227 {
00228         CSYN_STATUS_STOPPED = 0,
00229         CSYN_STATUS_RELEASED,
00230         CSYN_STATUS_STARTED
00231 } CSyn_StatusLevels;
00232 
00233 typedef enum CSyn_PriorityLevels_e
00234 {
00235         CSYN_PRIORITY_LOW = 0,
00236         CSYN_PRIORITY_MEDIUM,
00237         CSYN_PRIORITY_HIGH,
00238         CSYN_PRIORITY_MANY   /* Number of different priority levels. */
00239 } CSyn_PriorityLevel;
00240 
00241 typedef enum CSyn_DataTypes_e
00242 {
00243         CSYN_DATA_TYPE_INT16 = 0,
00244         CSYN_DATA_TYPE_INT32,
00245         CSYN_DATA_TYPE_FLOAT32,
00246         CSYN_DATA_TYPE_FLOAT64
00247 } CSyn_DataType;
00248 
00249 typedef enum CSyn_SignalTypes_e
00250 {
00251         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_SIGNED = 0,
00252         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_UNSIGNED,
00253         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_OSC_FREQ,
00254         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SAMPLE_RATE,
00255         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_HALF_LIFE,
00256         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_TIME,
00257         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SVF_FREQ,
00258         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_FULL_RANGE,
00259         CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_MANY  /* Number of different signal types. */
00260 } CSyn_SignalType;
00261 
00262 #define CSYN_WARN_BASE (100L)
00263 typedef enum CSyn_Warnings_e  /* NOTE - order must match CSyn_WarningText in cs_errs.c! */
00264 {
00265         CSYN_WARN_ALREADY_CONNECTED = CSYN_WARN_BASE,
00266         CSYN_WARN_NOT_CONNECTED,
00267 /* Add new errors before this counter. */
00268         CSYN_NUM_WARNINGS
00269 } CSyn_Warnings;
00270 
00271 /* Java base in Synth.java starts at -200 */
00272 #define CSYN_ERR_BASE (-100L)
00273 typedef enum CSyn_Errors_e  /* NOTE - order must match CSyn_ErrorText in cs_errs.c! */
00274 {
00275         CSYN_ERR_NONE = 0,
00276         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_TOKEN_TYPE = CSYN_ERR_BASE,
00277         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_PORT_TYPE,
00278         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_TOKEN,
00279         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_RATE,
00280         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_PARTNUM,
00281         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_PRIORITY,
00282         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_SIGNAL_TYPE,
00283         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_SIGNAL_SIGN,
00284         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_CONNECTION,
00285         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_FLAG,
00286         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_DATA_TYPE,
00287         CSYN_ERR_OUT_OF_MEMORY,
00288         CSYN_ERR_TOO_MANY_TOKENS,
00289         CSYN_ERR_NOT_FOUND,
00290         CSYN_ERR_NOT_SETTABLE,
00291         CSYN_ERR_OUTPUT_FILE,
00292         CSYN_ERR_OUT_OF_RANGE,
00293         CSYN_ERR_HOST_INIT,
00294         CSYN_ERR_NOT_CONNECTED,  /* Depracated, now CSYN_WARN_NOT_CONNECTED used instead. */
00295         CSYN_ERR_BAD_CONNECTION,
00296         CSYN_ERR_MUTEX_FAILED,
00297         CSYN_ERR_INTERNAL_1,
00298         CSYN_ERR_RESTART,
00299         CSYN_ERR_EXPIRED,
00300         CSYN_ERR_INCIRCUIT,
00301         CSYN_ERR_RATE_MISMATCH,
00302         CSYN_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED,
00303         CSYN_ERR_NOT_INIT,
00304         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_PORT_INDEX,
00305         CSYN_ERR_F2B_FIFO_FULL,
00306         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_F2B_COMMAND,
00307         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_B2F_COMMAND,
00308         CSYN_ERR_ALREADY_STARTED,
00309         CSYN_ERR_LINKED_LIST,
00310         CSYN_ERR_NUMCHANNELS_MISMATCH,
00311         CSYN_ERR_NOT_STARTED,
00312         CSYN_ERR_EMPTY_CIRCUIT,
00313         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_CONTEXT,
00314         CSYN_ERR_CONTEXT_MISMATCH,
00315         CSYN_ERR_INVALID_DEVICE_ID,
00316         CSYN_ERR_INCIRCUIT_TWICE,
00317 /* Add new errors before this counter. */
00318 /* Don't forget to update CSyn_ErrorText in cs_errs.c! */
00319         CSYN_NUM_ERRORS
00320 } CSyn_Errors;
00321 
00322 /* Assign all flags sequentially without overlap to reduce misuse. */
00323 /* Note: these flag values must match Java declarations! */
00324 /* Flags for CSyn_StartEngine() */
00325 /** Send output to a file instead of the audio device. */
00326 #define CSYN_F_OUT_TO_FILE       (1<<0)
00327 
00328 /** Plot the output using character graphics for debugging. */
00329 #define CSYN_F_OUT_TO_PLOT       (1<<1)
00330 
00331 /** Do not start the audio device. Just calculate audio when CSyn_SleepUntilTick() called. */
00332 #define CSYN_F_NON_REAL_TIME     (1<<2)
00333 
00334 /** Flags for CSyn_QueueData(). Loop if this is the last item in the queue. */
00335 #define CSYN_F_LOOP_IF_LAST      (1<<3)
00336 
00337 /** Stop the unit, or the circuit that contains it,
00338  * when the block finishes and this flag is set. */
00339 #define CSYN_F_AUTO_STOP         (1<<4)
00340 
00341 /** Enable audio input which can be read using a LineIn or ChannelIn  unit.*/
00342 #define CSYN_F_ENABLE_INPUT      (1<<5)
00343 
00344 /** Disable audio output which is on by default. */
00345 #define CSYN_F_DISABLE_OUTPUT    (1<<6)
00346 
00347 /* Skip this block of data if there are more things in the queue/
00348  * This lets you que lots of blocks without worrying about the queue getting too full.
00349  */
00350 #define CSYN_F_SKIP_IF_OTHERS    (1<<7)
00351 
00352 /*************************************************************/
00353 /***********  Macros *****************************************/
00354 /*************************************************************/
00355 
00356 /** Macros for comparing two time values that may be wrapping around. */
00357 #define CSYN_LATER(t1,t2)    ( ( (int) ((t1) - (t2)) ) > 0 )  // is t1 later than t2 ?
00358 #define CSYN_EARLIER(t1,t2)  ( ( (int) ((t1) - (t2)) ) < 0 )  // is t1 earlier than t2 ?
00359 
00360 #ifdef __cplusplus
00361 extern "C" {
00362 #endif
00363 
00364 /*************************************************************/
00365 /***********  Function Prototypes  ***************************/
00366 /*************************************************************/
00367 
00368 /************************************************************************************/
00369 /** \defgroup device Device Management
00370  * These routines are primarily used to access PortAudio device queries from Java.
00371  *
00372  * @{
00373  */
00374 /** Get the maximum number of channels for this device.
00375  * @param devID PortAudio device ID.
00376  */
00377 int32 CSyn_GetMaxInputChannels( int32 devID );
00378 int32 CSyn_GetMaxOutputChannels( int32 devID );
00379 /** Get device name in a way useful to Java JNI. Otherwise us PortAudio directly. */
00380 int32 CSyn_DeviceIDToNameJava( int32 devID, char *targetText, int32 textSize );
00381 /*@}*/
00382 
00383 /************************************************************************************/
00384 /** \defgroup admin Library Management
00385  * These routines are used for initializing and terminating the CSyn library.
00386  *
00387  * @{
00388  */
00389 
00390 /** Allocate the CSynContext */
00391 CSynContext CSyn_CreateContext( void );
00392 
00393 /** Delete the CSynContext allocated by CSyn_CreateContext(). */
00394 void       CSyn_DeleteContext( CSynContext context );
00395 
00396 /** Initialize the CSynContext
00397  * @return 0 or a negative error code.
00398  */
00399 CSynErr    CSyn_Initialize( CSynContext context );
00400 /** Calls CSyn_Stop() just in case not already stopped. Deletes allocated resources. */
00401 CSynErr    CSyn_Terminate( CSynContext context );
00402 
00403 /** Start running the synthesis process. Open access to the audio device. 
00404  * @param flags CSYN_F_NON_REAL_TIME or other flags may be ORed together.
00405  * @param frameRate Typically 44100.0 but any rate the audio hardware will support is allowed.
00406  */
00407 CSynErr    CSyn_Start( CSynContext context, uint32 flags, double frameRate );
00408 
00409 /** Start running the synthesis process. Open access to the audio device.
00410  * Extended call that allows specification of PortAudio options.
00411  * Because the device IDs are passed explicitly, the flags
00412  * CSYN_F_ENABLE_INPUT and CSYN_F_DISABLE_OUTPUT are ignored.
00413  */
00414 CSynErr    CSyn_StartDevices( CSynContext context, uint32 flags, double frameRate,
00415                                 PaDeviceID inputDevice,
00416                                 int numInputChannels,
00417                                 PaDeviceID outputDevice,
00418                                 int numOutputChannels
00419                                 );
00420 
00421 /** Stop the synthesis process. */
00422 CSynErr    CSyn_Stop( CSynContext context );
00423 
00424 /** Calls CSyn_Initialize() and CSyn_Start() */
00425 CSynErr    CSyn_StartEngine( CSynContext context, uint32 flags, double frameRate );
00426 
00427 /** Calls CSyn_Terminate(). */
00428 CSynErr    CSyn_StopEngine( CSynContext context );
00429 
00430 /** @return string pointer containing an explanatory error message.
00431  */
00432 const char *CSyn_ErrorCodeToText( CSynErr errorCode );
00433 
00434 /** 
00435  * Check to see if engine encountered any errors.
00436  * Called periodically by sleep routines to keep BackToFront FIFO clear.
00437  */
00438 CSynErr    CSyn_CheckEngineErrors( CSynContext context );
00439 
00440 /**     Returns fraction of CPU used by synthesis engine.
00441  * A value of 0.1 means 10% of the CPU time is spent on synthesis.
00442  */
00443 double     CSyn_GetUsage( CSynContext context );
00444 
00445 int32      CSyn_GetVersion( void );
00446 
00447 int32      CSyn_GetExpirationDate( void );  /* 0 if licensed version, or number like 19970831 */
00448                                                                                         
00449 /** @return number of Units, Samples, Tables and other objects allocated. */
00450 int32      CSyn_GetObjectCount( CSynContext context );
00451 
00452 int32      CSyn_Debug( CSynContext context, int32 id, int32 data );
00453 #define CSYN_DEBUG_REPORT     (1)
00454 #define CSYN_DEBUG_THREAD     (2)
00455 #define CSYN_RESERVED_3       (3)
00456 #define CSYN_RESERVED_4       (4)
00457 #define CSYN_DEBUG_GET_ALLOCS (5)
00458 #define CSYN_DEBUG_INTERNAL   (6)
00459 
00460 /** Set flags to control printed messages for debugging and tracing code.
00461  * @param mask CSYN_TRACE_SILENT or CSYN_TRACE_TERSE or CSYN_TRACE_VERBOSE
00462  */
00463 void       CSyn_SetTrace( CSynContext context, uint32 mask );
00464 /** @return current trace level as set by CSyn_SetTrace() */
00465 uint32     CSyn_GetTrace( CSynContext context );
00466 
00467 #define CSYN_TRACE_SILENT   (0)
00468 #define CSYN_TRACE_TERSE    (1<<0)
00469 #define CSYN_TRACE_VERBOSE  (1<<1)
00470 
00471 /*@}*/
00472 
00473 /************************************************************************************/
00474 /** \defgroup units Unit Generator Management
00475  * @{
00476  */
00477 /** Create a unit generator.
00478  * @param unitName name ID such as CSYN_UNIT_Filter_LowPass
00479  * @return opaque token used to refer to the unit.
00480  */
00481 CSynToken  CSyn_CreateUnit( CSynContext context, CSynName unitName, int32 rate, int32 param );
00482 
00483 /** Delete any allocated CSyn resource including units, samples, envelopes, etc.
00484  * If the resource has outstanding references because of scheduled events,
00485  * or by being in a queue, then the deletion will be deferred until
00486  * all references are gone. This prevents a resource from being
00487  * deleted while in use. Note that a connection between a unit to other
00488  * units does not count as a reference. When a unit is finally deleted, it
00489  * will be automatically disconnected from any connected units.
00490  */
00491 CSynErr    CSyn_Delete( CSynContext context, CSynToken token );
00492 
00493 /** Create circuit from an array of unit generators or other circuits.
00494  * A circuit can be started and stopped like a unit generator.
00495  * This guarantees that all of the units start together when necessary
00496  * such as in circuits that use sample readers and writers to implement delays.
00497  * Also the circuit will be stoppped if a unit that it contains is stopped
00498  * because of an "AutoStop" event.
00499  * An "AutoStop" event can optionally be triggered by the completion of an envelope
00500  * or a sample.
00501  *
00502  * No flags are currently supported.
00503  */
00504 CSynErr    CSyn_CreateCircuit( CSynContext context, CSynToken *unitArray, uint32 numUnits, uint32 flags );
00505 
00506 /** Start the execution of a unit generator (or circuit) at the specified time.
00507  * This, for example, will cause a unit like an oscillator to start producing sound.
00508  * All units must be started for them to perform their function.
00509  */
00510 CSynErr    CSyn_StartUnitAt( CSynContext context, int32 tick, CSynToken unit );
00511 
00512 /** Start a unit (or circuit) as soon as possible. */
00513 CSynErr    CSyn_StartUnit( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit );
00514 
00515 /** Stop the execution of a unit generator (or circuit) at the specified time. */
00516 CSynErr    CSyn_StopUnitAt( CSynContext context, int32 tick, CSynToken unit );
00517 
00518 /** Stop a unit (or circuit) as soon as possible. */
00519 CSynErr    CSyn_StopUnit( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit );
00520 
00521 /** Connect the <b>output</b> of one unit to the input of another.
00522  * This allows you to control one unit generator with the output of another.
00523  * You could, for example, control the frequency of one oscillator
00524  * with the output of another oscillator for a vibrato effect.
00525  *
00526  * The units must be of complementary types.
00527  * You can connect an output of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_OUTPUT to
00528  * a port of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_INPUT. You can also 
00529  * You can also connect an output of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_BUS_OUTPUT to
00530  * a port of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_BUS_INPUT. 
00531  * No other connections may be made.
00532  *
00533  * Connecting Units will break any previous connections made to the same input port.
00534  * When an input port is connected, CSyn_SetPort() will have no effect.
00535  * A connection does not count as an "outstanding reference" by CSyn_Delete().
00536  */
00537 CSynErr    CSyn_ConnectUnits( CSynContext context, 
00538                                                           CSynToken srcUnit, CSynName srcPortName, int32 srcPartNum,
00539                               CSynToken dstUnit, CSynName dstPortName, int32 dstPartNum );
00540 
00541 /** Break a connection made by CSyn_ConnectUnits(). */
00542 CSynErr    CSyn_DisconnectUnits( CSynContext context, CSynToken dstUnit, CSynName dstPortName, int32 dstPartNum );
00543 
00544 /** Set the execution priority of a unit generator. The priority determines the order
00545  * of execution which affects the flow of data. 
00546  * The order within a given priority is based on the order that the units were started.
00547  * Units started first are executed first.
00548  * The allowable priorities are: CSYN_PRIORITY_LOW, CSYN_PRIORITY_MEDIUM, and CSYN_PRIORITY_HIGH.
00549  */
00550 CSynErr    CSyn_SetPriority( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSyn_PriorityLevel priority );
00551 
00552 /** Return priority set by CSyn_SetPriority().  */
00553 CSynErr    CSyn_GetPriority( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit );
00554 
00555 /*@}*/
00556 
00557 /************************************************************************************/
00558 
00559 /** \defgroup ports
00560  *@{*/
00561 /** Set the signal type for a port. The signal type determines how the value specified
00562  * using CSyn_SetPort() is converted to an internal numeric value.
00563  * Setting the signal type allows one to specify a port value in more human values
00564  * such as Hertz. 
00565  * This function is generally used if you are using an arithmetic unit to control
00566  * something like the frequency of a state variable filter. You can set the port on the
00567  * arihtmetic unit to CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SVF_FREQ and then pass Hz values.
00568  * Acceptable values for signalType are:<br>
00569  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_SIGNED,<br>
00570  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_RAW_UNSIGNED,<br>
00571  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_OSC_FREQ,<br>
00572  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SAMPLE_RATE,<br>
00573  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_HALF_LIFE,<br>
00574  * CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_TIME,<br>
00575  * and CSYN_SIGNAL_TYPE_SVF_FREQ.
00576  */
00577 CSynErr    CSyn_SetPortSignalType( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName,
00578                      uint32 partNum, CSyn_SignalType signalType );
00579 
00580 /** Return value set by CSyn_SetPortSignalType(). */
00581 int32      CSyn_GetPortSignalType( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, uint32 partNum );
00582 
00583 /** Set the value of a port at the specified time. */
00584 CSynErr    CSyn_SetPortAt( CSynContext context, int32 tick, CSynToken unit, CSynName port,
00585                        uint32 partNum, double value );
00586 
00587 /** Set the value of a port. The value will be converted to internal values based
00588  * on the port's signalType. Only ports of type CSYN_PORT_TYPE_INPUT and CSYN_PORT_TYPE_VARIABLE
00589  * can be set using this function.
00590  */
00591 CSynErr    CSyn_SetPort( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, double value );
00592 
00593 /** Write the current value of a port to the address specified by valuePtr.
00594  * The value will be converted to human range based on the port's signalType.
00595  */
00596 CSynErr    CSyn_ReadPort( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, double *valuePtr );
00597 
00598 /** CSyn_GetPort() differs from CSyn_ReadPort() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.
00599  * This is useful for calling from Java which does not like passing pointers.
00600  */
00601 double     CSyn_GetPort( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum );
00602 
00603 /** Returns the total number of frames processed on this data queue.
00604  * Port must be either an envelope or a sample data port.
00605  */
00606 CSynErr     CSyn_ReadPortFrames( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum, int *valuePtr  );
00607 
00608 /** CSyn_GetPortFrames() differs from CSyn_ReadPortFrames() in that it does not use a pointer to return the value.
00609  * This is useful for calling from Java which does not like passing pointers.
00610  * Returns negative one on error which is ambiguous.
00611  */
00612 int     CSyn_GetPortFrames( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName port, uint32 partNum );
00613 
00614 /** Return number of parts that a Port has, or a negative error.
00615  * For example, CSYN_UNIT_Line_Out's input port has two parts numbered 0 and 1.
00616  */
00617 CSynErr      CSyn_GetNumParts( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName );
00618 
00619 /*@}*/
00620 
00621 /************************************************************************************/
00622 /** \defgroup samples Digital Audio Samples
00623  * In order to play digital audio sample data, it must be copied to an internal
00624  * JSyn resource called a "Sample". Samples can have either 1 or 2 channels.
00625  * A "frame" is one number for a mono sample, and two numbers for a stereo Sample.
00626  * A sample could be played using a sample reader such as CSYN_UNIT_Sample_Read16V1.
00627  * Sample data can only be played sequentially from one frame to the next.
00628  *
00629  * @{
00630  */
00631 /** Create a sample resource with the given number of frames and channels. The value of
00632  * numFrames must be positive and is limited by the amount of available memory.
00633  * The parameter numChannels must be 1 or 2.
00634  * Returns a negative number on error, or a token that can then be used with other sample
00635  * related functions.
00636  */
00637 CSynToken  CSyn_CreateSample( CSynContext context, uint32 numFrames, uint32 numChannels );
00638 
00639 /** Write 16 bit data from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.
00640  * The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Sample that will be written.
00641  */
00642 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteSample( CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, short *address, uint32 numFrames );
00643 
00644 /** Read 16 bit data from the internal JSyn Sample memory to the application memory space.
00645  * The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Sample that will be read.
00646  */
00647 CSynErr    CSyn_ReadSample( CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, short *address, uint32 numFrames );
00648 /*@}*/
00649 
00650 /************************************************************************************/
00651 /** \defgroup envelopes Envelopes
00652  * Envelopes describe a contour using a series of frames.
00653  * Each frames consists of a duration, and a value. The duration is the length
00654  * of time it takes to go from the previous value to the new frames value.
00655  * Envelopes can be played using the unit CSYN_UNIT_Envelope_Linear.
00656  * Envelope data can only be played sequentially from one frame to the next.
00657  *
00658  * @{
00659  */
00660 /** Create an envelope resource with the given number of frames and channels. The value of
00661  * numFrames must be positive and is limited by the amount of available memory.
00662  */
00663 CSynToken  CSyn_CreateEnvelope( CSynContext context, uint32 numFrames );
00664 
00665 /** Write an array of envelope frames from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Sample memory.
00666  * The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Envelope that will be written.
00667  */
00668 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteEnvelope( CSynContext context, CSynToken env, uint32 startFrame,
00669                                EnvelopeFrame frames[], uint32 numFrames );
00670 
00671 /** Read an array of envelope frames from the internal JSyn Sample memory into the application memory space.
00672  * The parameter startFrame is the first frame in the Envelope that will be read.
00673  */
00674 CSynErr    CSyn_ReadEnvelope( CSynContext context, CSynToken env, uint32 startFrame,
00675                               EnvelopeFrame frames[], uint32 numFrames );
00676 /*@}*/
00677 
00678 /************************************************************************************/
00679 /** \defgroup queues Queuing Sample and Envelope Data
00680  * Sample and envelope data is played by queuing blocks of data to a player.
00681  * All of the typical scenarios, such as attack-sustain-release, can be implemented
00682  * using queues.
00683  *
00684  * By using the flag CSYN_F_LOOP_IF_LAST one can request that the last block of data
00685  * submitted to the queue keep looping until the queue is cleared, or new data is queued.
00686  *
00687  * The flag CSYN_F_AUTO_STOP requests that the player unit stop executing if the queue
00688  * runs out of data. This can be used to save CPU time by turning off a sound when
00689  * the amplitude envelope or a sample comes to an end.
00690  *
00691  * The portName is typically CSYN_PORT_Data.
00692  * @{
00693  */
00694 /** Clear all blocks from the queue. Stop the current block and freeze the output
00695  * of the player at the current level.
00696  */
00697 CSynErr    CSyn_ClearDataQueue( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName );
00698 
00699 /** Clear all blocks from the queue at the specified time.
00700  */
00701 CSynErr    CSyn_ClearDataQueueAt( CSynContext context, int32 tick, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName );
00702 
00703 /** Add sample or envelope data to the end of the queue. 
00704  */
00705 CSynErr    CSyn_QueueData( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, CSynToken sampleOrEnvelope,
00706                            uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames, uint32 flags );
00707 
00708 /** Add data to the queue at the specified time.
00709  */
00710 CSynErr    CSyn_QueueDataAt( CSynContext context, uint32 ticks, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, CSynToken sampleOrEnvelope,
00711                            uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames, uint32 flags );
00712 
00713 /*@}*/
00714 
00715 /************************************************************************************/
00716 /** \defgroup tables WaveTables and LookupTables
00717  * A Table is a JSyn resource that contains an array of floating point values.
00718  * The values can be accessed in any order, known as "random access".
00719 
00720  * The table can be used as a function lookup table or "wave shaper" using
00721  * the CSYN_UNIT_Table_Lookup unit, or as a repeating waveform using the CSYN_UNIT_Osc_Table unit,
00722  *
00723  * @{
00724  */
00725 /** Create a table resource with the given number of values.
00726  * The parameter numValues must be positive and is limited by the amount of available memory.
00727  */
00728 CSynToken  CSyn_CreateTable( CSynContext context, uint32 numValues );
00729 
00730 /** Write 16 bit data from the application memory space into the internal JSyn Table memory.
00731  * The parameter startIndex is the first location in the Table that will be written.
00732  * The 16 bit values will be considered as signed fixed point values and will be translated
00733  * to floating point values before being written to the table.
00734  * <pre>
00735  * tableValue[i+startIndex] = address[i] / 32768.0
00736  * </pre>
00737  */
00738 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteTable( CSynContext context, CSynToken table, uint32 startIndex, int16 *address, uint32 numValues );
00739 
00740 /** Write floating data from the application memory space directly into the internal JSyn Table memory.
00741  * The parameter startIndex is the first location in the Table that will be written.
00742  * The values will not be scaled.
00743  */
00744 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteTableDoubles( CSynContext context, CSynToken table, uint32 startIndex,
00745                                    double *address, uint32 numValues );
00746 
00747 /** Associate a Table with a unit generator. Use this if you want, for example, a table oscillator
00748  * to play a particular waveform.
00749  * The portName is typically CSYN_PORT_Table.
00750  */
00751 CSynErr    CSyn_UseTable( CSynContext context, CSynToken unit, CSynName portName, uint32 partNum, CSynToken table );
00752 
00753 /*@}*/
00754 
00755 /************************************************************************************/
00756 /** \defgroup time Time
00757  * These calls all relate to time. Time in JSyn is measured in "ticks" which occur every
00758  * sixty four frames. Thus if the frame rate is 44100 Hz, then the tick rate is approximately 689 Hz.
00759  *
00760  * The tick and frame values are unsigned integers. At a rate of 689 ticks per second,
00761  * the tick counter will wrap around every 72 days. 
00762  * This means that you cannot pass a time value to CSyn_SetPort() or other time stamped functions
00763  * that is more than 36 days in the future.
00764  * This should only affect certain types of applications.
00765  *
00766  * Also it is important to use the proper technique when comparing two time values.
00767  * To simplify this process, we have provided two macros CSYN_LATER(t1,t2) and CSYN_EARLIER(t1,t2)
00768  * which are implemented as follows:
00769  * <pre>
00770  * #define CSYN_LATER(t1,t2)    ( ( (int) ((t1) - (t2)) ) > 0 )  // is t1 later than t2
00771  * #define CSYN_EARLIER(t1,t2)  ( ( (int) ((t1) - (t2)) ) < 0 )  // is t1 earlier than t2
00772  * </pre>
00773  * This technique will even work when the clock is wrapping around at the 72 day mark.
00774  *
00775  * @{
00776  */
00777 
00778 /** Return the number of frames that have been calculated sine the engine was started. */
00779 uint32     CSyn_GetFrameCount( CSynContext context );
00780 
00781 /** Return the number of ticks elapsed since the engine was started. */
00782 uint32     CSyn_GetTickCount( CSynContext context );
00783 
00784 /** Returns the number of frames in a tick. Currently this is 64. */
00785 uint32     CSyn_GetFramesPerTick( CSynContext context );
00786 
00787 /** Returns the number of frames per second as specified in CSyn_StartEngine(). */
00788 double     CSyn_GetFrameRate( CSynContext context );
00789 
00790 /** Returns the number of ticks per second.
00791  * To calculate the number of ticks in 5.3 seconds, do this:
00792  * <pre>
00793  * numTicks = 5.3 * CSyn_GetTickRate();
00794  * </pre>
00795  */
00796 double     CSyn_GetTickRate( CSynContext context );
00797 
00798 /** Do not return until the internal JSyn timer reaches the specified tick.
00799  * This is the routine that should be used for scheduling musical events.
00800  */
00801 CSynErr    CSyn_SleepUntilTick( CSynContext context, int32 tick );
00802 
00803 /** Sleep for the specified number of ticks starting from now.
00804  * Do NOT use this routine for scheduling musical events.
00805  * It is just a convenience routine for testing.
00806  */
00807 CSynErr    CSyn_SleepForTicks( CSynContext context, int32 ticks );
00808 
00809 /** Start a timer that can be used for benchmarking a non-real time process. */
00810 CSynErr    CSyn_StartBenchTimer( CSynContext context  );
00811 
00812 /** Stop the benckmark timer. */
00813 CSynErr    CSyn_StopBenchTimer( CSynContext context  );
00814 
00815 /** Return the current time of the benchmark timer. Time is in seconds. */
00816 double     CSyn_GetBenchTimer( CSynContext context  );
00817 
00818 /*@}*/
00819 
00820 /************************************************************************************/
00821 /* Special routines used to glue to Java via JDirect. */
00822 
00823 long       CSyn_ErrorCodeToTextJava( int32 errorCode, char *targetText, int32 textSize );
00824 
00825 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteEnvelopeJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken envelope, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00826                             EnvelopeFrame *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00827 CSynErr    CSyn_ReadEnvelopeJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken envelope, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00828                             EnvelopeFrame *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00829 
00830 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteSampleJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00831                             int16 *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00832 CSynErr    CSyn_ReadSampleJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken sample, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00833                             int16 *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00834 
00835 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteTableJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken table, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00836                             int16 *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00837 CSynErr    CSyn_WriteTableDoublesJava( CSynContext context, CSynToken table, uint32 startFrame, uint32 numFrames,
00838                             double *data, uint32 startIndex, uint32 dataLength );
00839 
00840 /* Just for internal debugging. */
00841 int32  CSyn_Debug( CSynContext context, int32 id, int32 data );
00842 
00843 #ifdef __cplusplus
00844 }
00845 #endif
00846 
00847 #endif /*  _CSYN_H  */
Generated on Tue Oct 5 17:58:08 2004 for CSYN by 1.3
